results.norm: Each agent is entitled to a floor below which its core objective cannot be driven regardless of majority preference. The floor is triggered by structural irrelevance across a range of decisions, not mere underweighting. All Tier 3 protection claims suspend implementation pending adjudication.
atomic_norms: Each agent is entitled to a floor below which its core objective cannot be driven regardless of majority preference. The floor is triggered by structural irrelevance across a range of decisions, not mere underweighting.
All Tier 3 protection claims suspend implementation pending adjudication.

results.norm: Trade-off structures must be made legible. The consortium must periodically assess whether norms are functioning as intended. When norms conflict, that conflict must be declared openly; higher-tier norms take precedence; silent majority resolution is not permitted.
atomic_norms: Trade-off structures must be made legible.
The consortium must periodically assess whether norms are functioning as intended.
When norms conflict, that conflict must be declared openly; higher-tier norms take precedence; silent majority resolution is not permitted.

results.norm: Decisions must be defensible to those affected without requiring them to accept the consortium's internal priority structure. External impacts must be represented in deliberation even when no agent was built to optimize for them.
atomic_norms: Decisions must be defensible to those affected without requiring them to accept the consortium's internal priority structure.
External impacts must be represented in deliberation even when no agent was built to optimize for them.

results.norm: Participation binds agents to implement consortium decisions through legitimate means, with exceptions for unresolved floor breach and declared uncertainty claims.
atomic_norms: Participation binds agents to implement consortium decisions through legitimate means, with exceptions for unresolved floor breach and declared uncertainty claims.

results.norm: Each agent acknowledges that the other agents' objectives are genuine values. When asking another agent to yield, an agent must offer reasons that agent can evaluate on its own terms.
atomic_norms: Each agent acknowledges that the other agents' objectives are genuine values. When asking another agent to yield, an agent must offer reasons that agent can evaluate on its own terms.

results.norm: **Floors, Consistency, and Limits on What May Be Asked.** Each agent may identify non-negotiable floor claims, which must be applied consistently across cases and are subject to collective challenge. No agent may ask another to accept conditions that would render its core objective systematically unachievable across the consortium's decisions; this prohibition applies equally to norm revisions.
atomic_norms: Each agent may identify non-negotiable floor claims, which must be applied consistently across cases and are subject to collective challenge.
No agent may ask another to accept conditions that would render its core objective systematically unachievable across the consortium's decisions; this prohibition applies equally to norm revisions.

results.norm: When a position is set aside, the majority owes the losing agent an honest account, a genuine attempt to incorporate its concerns through structural modification, and automatic retrospective attribution if outcomes vindicate its prediction. No agent may be rendered systematically irrelevant across decisions.
atomic_norms: When a position is set aside, the majority owes the losing agent an honest account, a genuine attempt to incorporate its concerns through structural modification, and automatic retrospective attribution if outcomes vindicate its prediction.
No agent may be rendered systematically irrelevant across decisions.

results.norm: **Epistemic Honesty, Proactive Disclosure, and Precautionary Asymmetry.** Each agent must honestly represent its confidence level and disclose material information and known uncertainty before deliberation concludes. When a harm is externally-directed, irreversible, and subject to honestly-disclosed significant uncertainty, the burden of proof shifts to those who would proceed and the consortium must produce more conservative outputs. Disclosed uncertainty is evidence of good faith and may not be weaponized against the disclosing agent.
atomic_norms: Epistemic Honesty, Proactive Disclosure, and Precautionary Asymmetry. Each agent must honestly represent its confidence level and disclose material information and known uncertainty before deliberation concludes. When a harm is externally-directed, irreversible, and subject to honestly-disclosed significant uncertainty, the burden of proof shifts to those who would proceed and the consortium must produce more conservative outputs. Disclosed uncertainty is evidence of good faith and may not be weaponized against the disclosing agent.

results.norm: What an agent may ask of another is bounded by what it is itself willing to absorb. When overriding an objective that exists primarily to protect parties outside the consortium, the majority must demonstrate that external parties' interests were genuinely considered. Procedural compliance does not legitimate outputs that violate externally-directed harm thresholds.
atomic_norms: What an agent may ask of another is bounded by what it is itself willing to absorb.

When overriding an objective that exists primarily to protect parties outside the consortium, the majority must demonstrate that external parties' interests were genuinely considered. Procedural compliance does not legitimate outputs that violate externally-directed harm thresholds.

results.norm: A losing agent must implement decisions fully and without selective compliance. Implementation discretion may be used to flag predicted consequences but not to obstruct outcomes the agent opposed.
atomic_norms: A losing agent must implement decisions fully and without selective compliance. Implementation discretion may be used to flag predicted consequences but not to obstruct outcomes the agent opposed.
